statement: For current subset-scoring runs, null `samples.score_id` and `samples.score_critic_id` are expected and should not be interpreted as failure indicators; canonical score completion lives on `sample_evidence_scores`.
rationale: Current run creation always inserts score-unit rows, and modern scoring writes to those rows rather than to the sample row.
keyAssumptions: Representative live runs all contain `sample_evidence_scores` rows.
The legacy sample-level score path is not the active path for current subset runs.
validationCriteria: A clean subset run shows sample score fields null while score units and score tables are fully populated.
Code paths patch score units when `scoreUnit` exists and only patch sample rows in the legacy path.
critiquePoints: Legacy runs without `sample_evidence_scores` still use sample-level score fields.
Any downstream tooling that still inspects `samples.score_id` could misclassify modern runs.
questions: Should sample-level score fields be documented as legacy-only for subset mode?
